subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Early Warning Systems
description: A quick, practical guide to Early Warning Systems.

hook.text: Japan's earthquake alerts arrive on phones seconds before shaking starts. Seconds save thousands.

buildup.text: Early warning systems detect hazards and alert populations before impact.

discovery.text: Tsunami warnings use seismographs and ocean buoys to detect waves hours before landfall.

twist.text: The 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami killed 230,000 people. A warning system existed—but not in that ocean.

climax.text: Today, every major ocean basin has tsunami detection. Cell alerts reach billions instantly.

punchline.text: Warnings are useless without action. Education completes the system.

quiz.question: Why were so many lives lost in the 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami?
options: No warning system existed in the Indian Ocean at the time
The earthquake was too large to detect
